channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hungary's Orbán predicts Trump will bring Russia back into Western fold after end of Ukraine war
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump's administration will reconnect Russia with the economies and energy networks of Western countries if the war in Ukraine, now nearly three years old, comes to an end, Hungary's nationalist prime minister said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Dem lawmakers answer USAID funding questions after bombshell report
item.description: Fox News Digital asked Democrat lawmakers their thoughts about controversial USAID funding, such as money ending up in the hands of terrorist-aligned groups.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Senate panel advances nomination of Kash Patel, Trump's pick to lead the FBI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate Judiciary Committee voted along party lines on Thursday to advance the nomination of Kash Patel, Donald Trump's pick for FBI director, pushing past Democratic concerns that he would operate as a loyalist for the president and target perceived adversaries of the White Hou
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Order to drop New York Mayor Adams' case roils Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams — a stunning escalation in a dayslong standoff over the Trump admini
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USAID's green energy programs have maximized harm to the developing world, according to former official
item.description: A former official at the U.S. Agency for International Development called out USAID's green energy programs as especially harmful for developing nations at a U.S. House hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Legislature cancels meetings, closes building as Sierra storm arrives in Nevada
item.description: While Las Vegas drivers navigate rain-soaked roads for the first time in awhile, the Nevada Legislature has shut down for the day to keep drivers out of hazardous conditions in the Reno-Carson City area.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A look at who might run for Sen. Tina Smith’s seat in the Senate
item.description: With Sen. Tina Smith’s announcement that she will not seek reelection in 2026, politicians on both sides of the aisle are eying her seat. Here are some of those who say they plan to run — or are at least considering it.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration was set to purchase $400 million worth of armored Teslas
item.description: That’s according to a public State Department procurement document. It comes as ethics experts raise conflict of interest questions about the chief executive of Tesla, Elon Musk, who is a top White House official.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. confirmed as Trump’s health secretary, over Democrats’ loud objections
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. wins confirmation to be President Trump’s secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services. The vote was 52 to 48.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin, MPR News

item.title: Judge orders Trump administration to temporarily allow funds for foreign aid to flow again
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to temporarily lift a three-week funding freeze that has shut down U.S. aid and development programs abroad.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst And Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Feds to probe Minnesota State High School League over transgender athlete policy
item.description: The Trump administration on Wednesday said it will investigate the league’s intention to follow state law and allow students in the state to participate in sports consistent with their gender identity, despite the president’s recent executive order.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Watch live: AG Bondi holds press conference as concerns rise over DOGE
item.description: Her remarks come as President Trump has faced criticism over the decision to fire several DOJ prosecutors and FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on RFK Jr. to lead HHS
item.description: The 53-47 vote came as Democrats have been forcing Republicans to use up all debate time on President Trump’s most controversial nominees.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Musk appear together in the Oval Office to defend the work of DOGE
item.description: Trump and Musk spoke as the president signed a new executive order calling on the heads of federal agencies to "promptly undertake preparations to initiate large-scale reductions in force.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: It’s the last week to change party affiliation ahead of NYC mayor’s race
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Friday is the last day to change your party registration ahead of the 2025 mayoral election. In June, New York City voters will pick their party’s candidate for the mayoral election in November.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs executive order restoring plastic straw use in government
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing the federal government to return to using plastic straws.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Charter Revision Commission to hold public hearings
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New Yorkers will get to voice their opinions on what amendments should be made to the city charter on Tuesday. Public hearings for the city's Charter Revision Commission will take place across all five boroughs in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: A timeline of US-China tit-for-tat tariffs since Trump's first term
item.description: China’s tit-for-tat duties on U.S. imports took effect Monday, just hours after U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he wants to slap new duties on all steel and aluminum imports to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he'll sign order promoting plastic straws
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he plans to sign an executive order promoting plastic straws after his predecessor sought to phase out single-use plastics from government operations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Members of Congress denied access to Department of Education
item.description: Multiple members of Congress were denied entry to the Department of Education on Friday, according to videos and social media posts from lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
